notes: big day for plant today. running 6 machines plus trucks. heres the rundown
so first up the 20 tonne excavator, CAT 320, Steves on it. started at 6:30 doing the basement dig in zone A. burning through diesel this things thirsty. filled it up last night, 200 litre tank
then we got the 8 tonne Kubota KX080, Mick operating. hes on the eastern trench work from 7. smaller machine works better in the tight areas near the boundary
the bobcat skid steer S650, Bluey operating. hes loading trucks all morning from the stockpile. switched to the pallet forks after lunch to move materials around
the 12 tonne smooth drum roller HAMM HD12, Dazza operating for the driveway sub base compaction. only needed from 9 to 12. 3 hours. compaction tests passed first go

got the mobile crane in this arvo, 25 tonne Franna AT-22. lifting the steel beams into the basement. 4 lifts total, heaviest was the main portal frame at 3.2 tonnes. crane on site from 1 to 4, 3 hours
and the concrete pump, 36 metre boom from Bills Concrete Pumping. just for the retaining wall pour this morning. on site 7 to 10, 3 hours. pumped 18 cubic metres total
trucks: 3 tip trucks carting spoil to the tip. Ryans Transport. averaging 6 loads each today. spoil going to the approved dump site on Johnson Rd

the 20 tonne excavator had a track tension issue around 10am. Steve adjusted it himself, took about 30 mins. said its been a bit loose for a few days. might need the mechanic to look at it properly on the weekend
the Kubota was down for about 45 mins after lunch. hydraulic hose blew on the dipper arm. lucky Mick had a spare hose in his toolbox, old school. got it swapped out and back running
the bobcat attachment swap from bucket to forks took about 15 mins around 12:30. also swapped back to bucket at 3pm for the last hour of cleanup. so two changeovers today about 15 mins each
end of day plant hours summary: CAT 320 did 8 hours, Kubota did 7 hours minus the 45 min breakdown so call it 6.25 productive hours, bobcat did 8 hours, roller 3 hours, crane 3 hours, pump 3 hours
all machines shut down and locked. keys in the site office. excavators parked in the dig area, bobcat near the shed, roller near the driveway

fuel situation: the 20 tonne needs a fill tomorrow for sure. the kubota is about half. bobcat is fine. gonna call the diesel supplier to bring the fuel truck out in the morning. need at least 300 litres

the hired roller is due back to Coates on Friday. only needed it for the driveway compaction. thats done now so might return it tomorrow if the boss is happy to save a days hire. at $380 a day its not cheap
crane hire invoice will be 3 hours minimum plus mob/demob. Franna quote was $180/hr plus $450 mob. so roughly $990 for today. worth it though, no way we could have lifted those beams by hand
